name: Go Modules Dependency Workflow
description: Manage Go module dependencies including adding, updating, removing packages, and handling version conflicts.

steps.title: Add a new dependency
steps.description: Install a package and add it to go.mod automatically.
steps.tip: Use @latest for the newest version, or @v1.9.0 to pin a specific version.

steps.title: Tidy dependencies
steps.description: Remove unused dependencies and add missing ones based on source code imports.

steps.title: List all dependencies
steps.description: View the full dependency tree including indirect dependencies.

steps.title: Check for available updates
steps.description: See which dependencies have newer versions available.
steps.tip: Pipe through grep to filter: go list -m -u all | grep '\[.*\]'

steps.title: Update a specific dependency
steps.description: Upgrade a single dependency to its latest minor or patch version.
steps.command: go get -u github.com/gin-gonic/gin && go mod tidy
steps.warning: The -u flag updates to the latest minor/patch. Use -u=patch to restrict to patch-level updates only.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Go Dependency Management
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Current Dependencies ==="
go list -m all | head -20

echo ""
echo "=== Available Updates ==="
go list -m -u all 2>/dev/null | grep '\[' || echo "All dependencies up to date"

echo ""
echo "=== Tidying Modules ==="
go mod tidy

echo ""
echo "=== Dependency Graph (direct) ==="
go mod graph | head -20

echo ""
echo "=== Module Commands ==="
echo "Add:       go get github.com/pkg/errors@latest"
echo "Update:    go get -u github.com/pkg/errors"
echo "Patch:     go get -u=patch github.com/pkg/errors"
echo "Remove:    Remove import, then go mod tidy"
echo "Vendor:    go mod vendor"
echo "Verify:    go mod verify"
echo "Why:       go mod why github.com/pkg/errors"

faq.question: How do I remove a dependency?
faq.answer: Remove all imports of the package from your source code, then run 'go mod tidy'. Go modules does not have an explicit remove command.

faq.question: What does 'go mod why' do?
faq.answer: It shows the shortest path from your module to a dependency, explaining why it is required. Useful for understanding indirect dependencies.

faq.question: How do I replace a dependency with a local fork?
faq.answer: Add a replace directive in go.mod: 'replace github.com/original/pkg => ../local-fork'. This is useful for testing changes before publishing.
